channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lawyers to File Impeachment Case Against Comelec Officials Over Election Irregularities
item.description: A group of Filipino lawyers has announced plans to file an impeachment complaint before the House of Representatives against Commission on Elections (Comelec) Chairperson George Garcia and six Commiss...

item.title: Iringa prepares to honour late William Lukuvi with a state funeral
item.description: Tanzania is set to hold a state funeral for the late William Vangimembe Lukuvi, former Minister of State in the Prime Minister’s Office, on March 31, 2026, at his home village of Idodi in Iringa Distr...

item.title: Tanzania's CUF Party Navigates Leadership Transition and Internal Disputes
item.description: Tanzania's Chama Cha Umoja na Utaifa (CUF) party is currently facing a period of transition and internal conflict following the formal departure of its longtime leader, Prof. Ibrahim Lipumba. During a...

item.title: Spain Restricts U.S. Military Access Amid Tensions Over Iran Conflict
item.description: Spain has formally closed its airspace to U.S. military planes involved in attacks on Iran, extending its previous denial of base usage for such operations. The Spanish government emphasized that this...

item.title: PM Shehbaz outlines relief and austerity measures to address fuel crisis impact
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has stated that the government is actively implementing relief measures to support Pakistan’s lower and middle-income groups as global fuel prices surge because of the on...

item.title: President Zardari and PM Shehbaz discuss national security and regional developments
item.description: On Monday, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif met with President Asif Ali Zardari to assess the overall national security situation and key challenges facing Pakistan. The meeting came shortly after a quad...

item.title: Prime Video unveils ‘Crime 101’ trailer starring Hemsworth, Berry and Ruffalo
item.description: The much-anticipated trailer for 'Crime 101' has been released, offering audiences a first glimpse at Bart Layton’s adaptation of Don Winslow’s novella. This heist thriller features an impressive cast...

item.title: Larry Madowo honoured for bold reporting on East African human rights struggles
item.description: Kenyan journalist Larry Madowo has been named the Human Rights Defender of the Year during the 10th anniversary of the Human Rights Defenders (HRD) Awards. The recognition celebrates his fearless cove...

item.title: Highlighting Women’s Role in Coping with Climate Change in Tanzania
item.description: In Tanzania, women are facing the biggest burden from climate change even though their contributions remain largely unnoticed in policy making and development planning. Most women in rural and urban c...

item.title: Turbo Energy projects strong FY 2025 revenue growth, shares increase by 13%
item.description: Turbo Energy, S.A. (TURB) announced its preliminary, unaudited revenue forecast for fiscal year 2025, expecting total revenue to fall between $22.5 million and $23.5 million. This represents a substan...

item.title: Russia orders British diplomat to leave over alleged espionage
item.description: Russia has expelled a British diplomat, accusing him of providing false information to gain entry to the country and attempting to obtain sensitive intelligence during informal economic meetings. The ...

item.title: Kīlauea Volcano Summit Eruption Remains Paused; Next Episode Expected in Early April
item.description: The Kīlauea volcano summit eruption on Hawaii's Big Island continues to be paused, according to the latest update from the USGS Hawaiian Volcano Observatory (HVO). Current monitoring indicates that th...

item.title: How the Middle East Conflict is Affecting the Philippines' Fuel, Economy, and OFWs
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the United States and Israel against Iran has had significant repercussions for the Philippines, a country that relies heavily on the Middle East for its oil supply. The g...

item.title: Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie Will Not Attend Royal Easter Service at Windsor
item.description: Princess Beatrice and Princess Eugenie, daughters of Andrew, Duke of York, will not be present at the royal family's traditional Easter church service at Windsor Castle this year. According to reports...

item.title: G7 commits to coordinated action to stabilise energy markets amid Iran conflict
item.description: Finance leaders from the G7 countries, which include the US, Canada, Japan, the UK, France, Germany, and Italy, have stated their readiness to implement all necessary measures to maintain stability in...

item.title: Russell Brand’s trial over multiple sexual assault charges postponed until October
item.description: The trial of actor and comedian Russell Brand, accused of multiple counts of rape and sexual assault, has been postponed until 12 October. The 50-year-old faces seven allegations, including three coun...

item.title: Supporting Informal Traders in South Africa: Five Policy Priorities
item.description: Informal trading plays a crucial role in South Africa’s economy, particularly in providing livelihoods for marginalised communities and bringing essential goods and services closer to residents. Despi...

item.title: Weekly podcast roundup featuring a new Serial production and diverse storytelling highlights
item.description: This weekly podcast roundup highlights a range of new and noteworthy audio series, led by a fresh release connected to the acclaimed Serial Productions. The standout recommendation, “The Idiot”, is ho...

item.title: Kenya Faces Scrutiny Over KSh 6.3 Billion eCitizen Funds Transfer
item.description: The Office of the Auditor General in Kenya has flagged a serious issue involving KSh 6.3 billion allegedly transferred from the government's eCitizen platform to a private bank account without Nationa...

item.title: LTFRB orders 8 bus companies to explain unauthorized fare increases
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) has issued show cause orders (SCOs) against eight bus companies for allegedly raising fares without government approval. LTFRB Chair Vi...
